steps.title: Identify the goal and what the audience already knows
steps.content: The student wants to describe where Fung is positioned in the photograph. The audience is already familiar with both Kang and Fung, so biographical details about either artist (who they are, where Kang is based) are irrelevant. The answer must focus on Fung's location in the frame.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Dominique Fung is in a photograph by Mary Kang, a portrait photographer based in New York City and Austin, Texas.': States that Fung appears in the photo but says nothing about where Fung is positioned; wastes words on Kang's biography the audience already knows. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Mary Kang is a photographer based in both New York City and Austin, Texas.': Describes Kang, not Fung's location in the photograph at all. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Five paintings by artist Dominique Fung can be seen in the background of Mary Kang's photograph.': Focuses on the paintings in the background rather than on where Fung herself is positioned. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In Kang's portrait of her, Fung is seated on the floor, with five of her paintings resting against the wall behind her.': Directly describes Fung's position (seated on the floor) without any unnecessary biographical information. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
